articles.title: NASA rolls out Artemis 3 SLS rocket's huge core stage to gear up for 2027 launch (photo)
articles.description: NASA rolled the Artemis 3 core stage out from the Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans on Monday (April 20). The next stop is the launch site: Kennedy Space Center in Florida.

articles.title: Artemis spacesuit development risks further delays
articles.description: New spacesuits for Artemis lunar missions and the International Space Station may not be ready until after the end of the decade, a report by NASA’s inspector general warns. The post Artemis spacesuit development risks further delays appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: NASA on Track for Future Missions with Initial Artemis II Assessments
articles.description: Following NASA’s Artemis II mission successfully splashing down on Earth, engineers started diving into detailed analysis of data to assess how key systems and subsystems on the Orion spacecraft, SLS (Space Launch System) rocket, and systems at the launch pad at the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in

articles.title: NASA Rolls Out Artemis III Moon Rocket Core Stage
articles.description: Following the recent successful test flight of NASA’s Artemis II mission around the Moon, NASA rolled out the core stage, or the largest section, of the agency’s SLS (Space Launch System) rocket that will launch the crewed Artemis III mission in 2027. The stage departed from the agency’s Michoud Ass

articles.title: Latvia joins the Artemis Accords
articles.description: Latvia is the latest country to sign the Artemis Accords as part of a new push to use the Accords to foster cooperation on NASA’s lunar exploration ambitions. The post Latvia joins the Artemis Accords appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: NASA Welcomes Latvia as Newest Artemis Accords Signatory
articles.description: The Republic of Latvia signed the Artemis Accords Monday during a ceremony hosted by NASA at the agency’s headquarters in Washington, becoming the 62nd nation to commit to responsible space exploration for all humanity.  “We are proud to welcome Latv

articles.title: A peek at the far side of the moon | Space photo of the day for April 20, 2026
articles.description: The Artemis 2 astronauts captured a unique image of the moon's "dark side" during their historic April 2026 flyby.

articles.title: Space Force sets up ‘cislunar coordination’ office to focus beyond Earth orbit
articles.description: Officials say civil-military overlap with NASA grows as lunar activity accelerates The post Space Force sets up ‘cislunar coordination’ office to focus beyond Earth orbit appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: In the wake of Artemis 2, America needs to consider the ‘why’ of its government space program
articles.description: The moon itself does not wax and wane as frequently as American public opinion on its space program. The Artemis 2 astronauts barely gulped their first taste of fresh Pacific air before a chorus of voices began criticizing the Artemis program as a waste of money for which the American people receive

articles.title: Pragmata's tale of AI slop, humanity, & lunar conquest makes it the timeliest sci-fi game of the year
articles.description: Capcom's new IP is a welcome throwback to simpler action games, but its story and themes are shockingly well-timed and refreshingly optimistic.

articles.title: Artemis 2 and Tiangong space station astronauts set record for farthest distance between humans
articles.description: For a few moments on April 6, the four Artemis 2 moon astronauts and the three crewmates aboard China's Tiangong space station were farther away from each other than any humans had ever been.

articles.title: Artemis 2 astronauts praise performance of Orion
articles.description: The astronauts who flew around the moon on Artemis 2 said they were confident the Orion spacecraft is ready to support future missions. The post Artemis 2 astronauts praise performance of Orion appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Artemis 2's heat shield seems to have aced its trial by fire
articles.description: The heat shield on Artemis 2's Orion capsule appears to have held up incredibly well during its scorching downward trip through Earth's atmosphere, mission commander Reid Wiseman said.

articles.title: Landing astronauts on the moon 'is absolutely doable, and it's doable soon,' NASA's Artemis 2 commander says
articles.description: The Artemis 2 astronauts said they gained a deeper understanding of their Orion capsule during their moon mission —and what they learned bodes well for future Artemis flights.

articles.title: 'That was a wonderful toilet': Artemis 2 commander defends mission's lunar loo
articles.description: Artemis 2's groundbreaking space toilet doesn't deserve the bad press it's gotten over the past two weeks, according to mission commander Reid Wiseman.

articles.title: Max Space unveils new expandable space habitat for the moon and beyond: 'We need real estate that is scalable'
articles.description: "They are truly ready for long duration space flights and lunar habitation."

articles.title: A new Earthrise: An Apollo historian experiences Artemis 2
articles.description: As Artemis 2 approached, my anticipation was mixed with uncertainty. Would this new Moon mission spark the wonder and excitement I'd felt during Apollo? Those doubts didn't last long.
